name: Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar
name_original: Embalse de Contreras — ida y vuelta desde Motilla de Palancar
description: This difficult 49.9-mile touring cycling route from Motilla de Palancar explores the stunning Embalse de Contreras and Cabriel Valley.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Motilla del Palancar offers routes through varied landscapes in the province of Cuenca, Spain. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Alarcón Reservoir, formed by the Júcar River, which provides a notable natural feature. Surrounding areas feature diverse natural spaces and lush greenery, creating suitable conditions for cycling. The terrain includes both flat sections and areas with elevation changes, accommodating different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Motilla Del Palancar The most popular touring cycling route is Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar, a 49.9 miles (80.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and passes by two reservoirs. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Paracuellos y Almodóvar del Pinar — circular desde Motilla del Palancar, a difficult 36.8 miles (59.3 km) path. This circular route offers varied scenery through the local countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Alarcón — circular from Motilla de Palancar, a 35.3 miles (56.9 km) trail leading through the area around the Alarcón Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Motilla del Palancar is defined by routes near the Alarcón Reservoir, along the Júcar River, and through diverse natural spaces. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 9 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Motilla del Palancar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes can I find around Motilla del Palancar?
A: Motilla del Palancar offers a variety of touring cycling routes, ranging from easy to difficult. You'll find options that explore the local countryside, pass by the Alarcón Reservoir, and follow the Júcar River. The terrain includes both flatter sections and areas with significant elevation changes, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several popular touring cycling routes around Motilla del Palancar are circular. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365716918/paracuellos-y-almodovar-del-pinar-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos y Almodóvar del Pinar — circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; covers 59.3 km, offering varied scenery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086241247/alarcon-circular-from-motilla-de-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón — circular from Motilla de Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 56.9 km and leads through the area around the Alarcón Reservoir.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes feature the prominent &lt;strong&gt;Alarcón Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, formed by the Júcar River, which is a central point for outdoor recreation. You might also encounter diverse natural spaces and lush greenery. Specific landmarks include the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6266927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Tres Ojos&lt;/a&gt; at the entrance to Motilla del Palancar, and the 17th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Loma&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Experienced touring cyclists can tackle routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086060804/hoces-del-cabriel-and-contreras-reservoir-loop-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 80.3 km trail with over 850 meters of elevation gain, passing by two reservoirs. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365716918/paracuellos-y-almodovar-del-pinar-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos y Almodóvar del Pinar — circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 59.3 km path.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Motilla del Palancar?
A: While specific seasonal closures are not noted, the broader Cuenca region, where Motilla del Palancar is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures, making longer rides more comfortable than the heat of summer.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, intermediate riders have several moderate options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363900017/iniesta-circular-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iniesta — circular from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; is a 67.0 km route with around 400 meters of elevation gain. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Motilla del Palancar – Gabaldón loop from Camino Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a shorter 31.5 km loop with about 250 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Motilla del Palancar?
A: The touring cycling routes around Motilla del Palancar are explored by a growing community. Over 9 touring cyclists have used komoot to discover the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the options available for different skill levels, particularly those routes near the Alarcón Reservoir.

Q: Are there any cultural attractions in Motilla del Palancar that I can visit after a ride?
A: Yes, Motilla del Palancar offers cultural points of interest. You can visit the 16th-century &lt;em&gt;rollo&lt;/em&gt; in Parque del Carrascal, the charming 16th-century Ermita de la Inmaculada Concepción, or the significant Iglesia de San Gil Abad built between the 16th and 17th centuries. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Loma&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable 17th-century site.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Motilla del Palancar area?
A: There are 7 touring cycling routes documented in the Motilla del Palancar area, offering a range of experiences from easy to difficult, ensuring options for various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Is the terrain varied for touring cycling around Motilla del Palancar?
A: Yes, the terrain is quite varied. You'll find routes that traverse both relatively flat sections and areas with notable elevation changes. This diversity allows for different cycling experiences, from more relaxed rides through the countryside to challenging climbs with scenic views over the Alarcón Reservoir.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners or casual riders?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available, making the area accessible for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing for a comfortable exploration of the local scenery.
tips:
Viewpoint next to the old N-III highway
After passing the engineer's house, a turnoff leads to the viewpoint located above the old N-III highway.
Sweeping views of the reservoir from a high, accessible point.

This viewpoint is located on the CV-4661 highway near the Contreras reservoir. From this point we can enjoy the views of Peñablanca (viewpoint located on a large rock) and Los Cuchillos (limestone ridges). You can also distinguish the Cabriel River and the Contreras Dam. The viewpoint has an information panel.

Dam on the Cabriel River completed in 1972, 129 m high and 241 m long. The dam forms the Contreras reservoir and makes it possible to produce energy and supply water to the Júcar-Cabriel basin. Due to seepage problems in the rock, the reservoir will never be able to reach 100% capacity.
The Cabriel river marks the limit between the provinces of Cuenca and Valencia as well as between the Communities of Castilla La Mancha and the Valencian Community respectively.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for this route?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route is estimated to take approximately 5 hours to complete. However, actual time may vary depending on your fitness level, pace, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as difficult and requires a very good level of fitness. It is not recommended for beginners due to its length, significant elevation gain, and potentially challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Contreras — round trip from Motilla de Palancar a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, this is a round trip route, meaning it forms a loop starting and ending in Motilla de Palancar.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route likely features a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. Given its 'difficult' rating, expect challenging unpaved segments that may require strong cycling skills or even dismounting and pushing your bike. Some parts might be well-maintained dirt tracks, but technical sections are probable.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect spectacular and impressive scenery, including stunning views of the Embalse de Contreras with its clear, turquoise waters. The route also offers vistas of the Cabriel Valley and the dramatic 'Cuchillares de las Hoces del Cabriel' rock formations within the Natural Park. The landscape is characterized by natural beauty, vineyards, and hills.

faqs.question: What notable sights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the Contreras Viewpoint, offering panoramic views, the Cabriel Valley Viewpoint, and the impressive Contreras Dam (Cabriel River). The route also passes through the unique geological formations known as the 'Cuchillos del Cabriel'.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with other paths, including the Ruta de Los Calderones. Minglanilla, Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Alicante, Sendero de Los Cuchillos del Cabriel, Camino del Castellar, Calle Pozo Arriba, Camino del cementerio, Camino El Perogilo, Cañada Real de los Serranos, and Camino de Mascahierros.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations for this exact route are not available, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant cycling conditions in this region, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential colder, wetter weather in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Contreras cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The information available does not explicitly state whether this specific cycling route is dog-friendly. However, as it passes through the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park, it's advisable to check the park's specific regulations regarding dogs, especially leash requirements, to ensure compliance and safety for your pet and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this route or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for cycling this route itself. As the route is located within the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park, there are typically no entrance fees for the park. However, always check official park websites for any updated regulations or specific activity permits that might be required.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling trip?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Motilla de Palancar. You should be able to find public parking within the town to begin your cycling trip. Look for designated parking areas or consult local maps upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay overnight near the route?
faqs.answer: Motilla de Palancar, being the starting and ending point, offers amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options suitable for cyclists. It's recommended to check local listings in Motilla de Palancar for specific establishments.
